name: datalayer-jupyter-vscode
description: ✨ AI ⚡ GPU 👥 Collaboration for 🪐 Jupyter Notebooks - 📄 Notion-like editor included.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run clean && npm run package
scripts.package: npm run clean:dist && webpack --mode production --devtool hidden-source-map
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
scripts.check: npm run format:check && npm run lint && npm run type-check
scripts.check:fix: npm run format && npm run lint:fix && npm run type-check

commands.title: Attach Current Document to Copilot Chat
Test: Get Active Document (Show What Copilot Sees)

viewsWelcome.contents:
Welcome to Datalayer Spaces.
[Login to Datalayer](command:datalayer.login) to view your spaces and documents.

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_getActiveDocument
languageModelTools.modelDescription: **IMPORTANT: Call this tool FIRST when the user is viewing a .ipynb or .lexical file in VS Code.** Returns the complete content of custom editor documents (Jupyter Notebooks and Lexical documents) that are NOT visible through normal VS Code text editor APIs. Standard file reading will NOT work for these custom editors - you MUST use this tool. Returns: full document content (JSON structure with cells/nodes), file name, URI, type (notebook/lexical/other), scheme, and line count. Use this whenever the user asks about 'this notebook', 'this file', 'current document', or any question that requires seeing the content of an open .ipynb or .lexical file.

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_insertCell
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Inserts a code or markdown cell into a Jupyter notebook at a specified position or at the end

cellType.description: Type of cell to insert: 'code' for executable Python code or 'markdown' for formatted text

source.description: Content of the cell (Python code or Markdown text)

index.description: Optional: Position where the cell should be inserted (0-based index). If not provided, inserts at the end.

uri.description: Optional: Document identifier. If not provided, uses the active notebook editor.

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_deleteCell
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Deletes a cell from a Jupyter notebook at the specified index

index.description: Index of the cell to delete (0-based)

uri.description: Optional: Document identifier. If not provided, uses the active notebook.

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_updateCell
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Updates (overwrites) a cell's source code at the specified index. Does NOT execute the cell.

index.description: Index of the cell to update (0-based)

source.description: New source code for the cell

uri.description: Optional: Document identifier. If not provided, uses the active notebook.

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_readCell
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reads a specific cell from a Jupyter notebook by index, including source code and outputs

index.description: Index of the cell to read (0-based)

uri.description: Optional: Document identifier. If not provided, uses the active notebook.

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_readAllCells
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reads all cells from a Jupyter notebook, including source code and outputs for each cell

uri.description: Optional: Document identifier. If not provided, uses the active notebook.

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_executeCell
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Executes a code cell in a Jupyter notebook and returns its outputs

index.description: Index of the cell to execute (0-based, must be a code cell)

uri.description: Optional: Document identifier. If not provided, uses the active notebook.

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_getNotebookInfo
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Gets metadata about a Jupyter notebook including path, cell counts, and kernel information

uri.description: Optional: Document identifier. If not provided, uses the active notebook.

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_readBlocks
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read all blocks from the currently open Lexical document. Returns array of blocks, each with: block_id (stable identifier for insertion), block_type (e.g. 'heading', 'paragraph', 'jupyter-cell'), source (content as string), metadata (properties like level, language). CRITICAL: Use the block_id values from this result for insertBlock's insert_after_block_id parameter. Works on active .lexical file.

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_insertBlock
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Insert blocks into Lexical documents (.lexical files). CRITICAL: Use exact parameter names shown below. ADDRESSING: Required parameter 'insertAfter' (NOT 'index'). WORKFLOW: 1) Call readBlocks to get block_id values, 2) Use insertAfter='TOP' for beginning, 'BOTTOM' for end, or actual block_id to insert after that block. EXAMPLE: {insertAfter: 'BOTTOM', type: 'jupyter-cell', source: 'print(1+1)', properties: {language: 'python'}}

insertAfter.description: REQUIRED. Insert position: 'TOP' (beginning), 'BOTTOM' (end), or block_id from readBlocks. Example: 'BOTTOM' or '7'.

source.description: REQUIRED. Block content as string. Example: 'print(1+1)'

properties.description: Optional metadata object. Put language HERE: {"language": "python"} for code/jupyter-cell, {"level": 1} for h1 heading. Do NOT put language at top level.

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_insertBlocks
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Insert multiple blocks into Lexical documents (.lexical files) in a single efficient operation. Use this instead of multiple insertBlock calls when creating documents with many blocks. ADDRESSING: First block inserts after 'insertAfter', subsequent blocks follow in order. WORKFLOW: 1) Prepare array of blocks with type, source, and optional properties, 2) Call insertBlocks with insertAfter='TOP' for beginning, 'BOTTOM' for end, or block_id from readBlocks. EXAMPLE: {insertAfter: 'BOTTOM', blocks: [{type: 'heading', source: 'Title', properties: {level: 1}}, {type: 'jupyter-cell', source: 'print(1)', properties: {language: 'python'}}]}

insertAfter.description: REQUIRED. Insert position for first block: 'TOP' (beginning), 'BOTTOM' (end), or block_id from readBlocks. Subsequent blocks follow in order.

blocks.description: REQUIRED. Array of blocks to insert. Each block must have type and source. Blocks are inserted sequentially.

items.description: A single block to insert with its type, content, and optional metadata.

properties.description: Optional metadata. Examples: {"language": "python"} for code/jupyter-cell, {"level": 1} for h1 heading.

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_deleteBlock
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Delete a block from the currently open Lexical document by its block_id. WORKFLOW: 1) Call readBlocks to get block_id values, 2) Pass the block_id of the block to delete. This permanently removes the block and changes appear immediately.

id.description: ID of the block to delete (from block_id field in readBlocks).

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_listAvailableBlocks
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Discover available block types for the currently open Lexical document. Returns schema for all registered blocks including: 'jupyter-cell' (executable code cell with language property), standard blocks (paragraph, heading, code, quote, list, table). Use this to see exact block type names and required properties before calling insertBlock.

category.description: Optional category filter. Categories: "text" (paragraph, quote), "heading" (h1-h6), "code" (code blocks), "list" (bullet/numbered), "table", "media" (plots), "special" (jupyter-cell, dataframe-viewer). Omit to get all block types.

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_createNotebook
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Creates a new Jupyter notebook with smart location detection. Automatically determines whether to create locally or in cloud based on context (keywords, open notebooks, space mentions, auth state). Use keywords like 'cloud', 'remote', 'space' for cloud notebooks, or 'local', 'workspace' for local notebooks.

name.description: Name of the notebook (e.g., 'data-analysis' or 'analysis.ipynb'). Extension '.ipynb' is added automatically if missing.

description.description: Optional: Description of the notebook's purpose (e.g., 'for analyzing sales data')

space.description: Optional: Name of the Datalayer cloud space (e.g., 'Personal', 'Team Project'). If specified, notebook will be created in cloud. Defaults to 'Personal' if not specified.

location.description: Optional: Explicit location override. Use 'local' for workspace or 'cloud' for Datalayer space. If not specified, location is auto-detected from context.

languageModelTools.name: datalayer_createLexical
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Creates a new Lexical document with smart location detection. Automatically determines whether to create locally or in cloud based on context (keywords, open documents, space mentions, auth state). Use keywords like 'cloud', 'remote', 'space' for cloud documents, or 'local', 'workspace' for local documents.

name.description: Name of the lexical document (e.g., 'meeting-notes' or 'notes.lexical'). Extension '.lexical' is added automatically if missing.

description.description: Optional: Description of the document's purpose (e.g., 'for team meeting notes')

space.description: Optional: Name of the Datalayer cloud space (e.g., 'Personal', 'Team Project'). If specified, document will be created in cloud. Defaults to 'Personal' if not specified.

location.description: Optional: Explicit location override. Use 'local' for workspace or 'cloud' for Datalayer space. If not specified, location is auto-detected from context.

datalayer.services.iamUrl.description: IAM (Identity and Access Management) service URL

datalayer.services.runtimesUrl.description: Runtimes service URL for computational environments

datalayer.services.spacerUrl.description: Spacer service URL for workspaces and collaboration

datalayer.services.spacerWsUrl.description: WebSocket URL for Spacer real-time collaboration

datalayer.runtime.defaultMinutes.description: Default runtime duration in minutes (max 24 hours)

datalayer.autoConnect.strategies.markdownDescription:
Auto-connect strategies for opening notebooks and lexical documents. Strategies are tried in order until one succeeds:

- **Active Runtime**: Select the runtime with the most time remaining from available runtimes
- **Ask**: Show a Quick Pick to select from available runtimes
- **Empty array `[]`**: Disable auto-connect (manual selection required)

**Examples:**
- `["Active Runtime"]` - Only connect to runtime with most time, otherwise no kernel
- `["Active Runtime", "Ask"]` - Try runtime with most time first, then ask user if none available
- `["Ask"]` - Always ask user to select runtime
- `[]` - No auto-connect, user must manually select kernel

enumDescriptions: Most verbose - includes all debug information and operation traces
Verbose - includes detailed debugging information for troubleshooting
Standard - includes important events and status updates
Important - includes warnings and potential issues
Critical - includes only errors and critical failures
datalayer.logging.level.description: Controls the minimum log level for extension logging output

datalayer.logging.includeTimestamps.description: Include timestamps in log messages for better debugging

datalayer.logging.includeContext.description: Include additional context information (operation IDs, request details) in logs

datalayer.logging.enableSDKLogging.description: Enable comprehensive logging for DatalayerClient SDK operations and API calls

datalayer.logging.enablePerformanceMonit.description: Enable performance monitoring with timing and memory usage tracking (may impact performance)

datalayer.onboarding.showWelcome.markdownDescription: Show unified welcome prompt on first activation with recommended configuration (default editor + Jupyter tools optimization)

enumDescriptions: Standard JSON format - structured data optimized for programmatic parsing
TOON format - human/LLM-readable format with compact, intuitive syntax
datalayer.tools.responseFormat.markdownDescription: Format for MCP tool responses. **TOON** provides more readable output for LLMs, while **JSON** maintains traditional structured format.

chatParticipants.name: datalayer
chatParticipants.description: AI assistant specialized in Datalayer notebooks and lexical documents. Type @datalayer or @d to invoke. Automatically understands .ipynb and .lexical files.

disambiguation.description: Datalayer AI assistant for notebooks and lexical documents

commands.name: explain
commands.description: Explain the structure and content of the current document

commands.name: summarize
commands.description: Provide a summary of the document content

commands.name: sections
commands.description: Show the main sections or cells in the document

walkthroughs.title: Getting Started with Datalayer
walkthroughs.description: Learn how to use Datalayer to create cloud-powered Jupyter notebooks and collaborative documents in VS Code.

steps.title: Login to Datalayer App
steps.description:
Create your Datalayer account to access cloud-powered Jupyter notebooks with GPU support and real-time collaboration.

Visit [datalayer.app](https://datalayer.app/) and login with **GitHub** or **LinkedIn** to get started.

media.altText: Login to Datalayer App with GitHub or LinkedIn

steps.title: Generate Your API Key
steps.description:
Your API Key is required to securely connect VS Code to your Datalayer account.

After logging in, go to [Settings > IAM > Tokens](https://datalayer.app/settings/iam/tokens) to create your **API Key**. Keep it secure as you'll use it to authenticate VS Code.

media.altText: Generate API Key in Datalayer Settings

steps.title: Connect VS Code to Datalayer
steps.description:
Connect VS Code to your Datalayer account to access cloud runtimes, shared workspaces, and collaboration features.

Click the button below and paste your **API Key** when prompted.

[Login to Datalayer](command:datalayer.login)

media.altText: Connect VS Code to Datalayer - API Key Input Dialog

steps.title: Create Your First Notebook
steps.description:
Jupyter notebooks combine code, visualizations, and documentation in one interactive document. Perfect for data analysis, machine learning, and scientific computing.

[Create Local Notebook](command:datalayer.newLocalDatalayerNotebook)

[Create Notebook in Space](command:datalayer.newRemoteDatalayerNotebookPrompt)

media.altText: Jupyter notebook editor with code cells and outputs

steps.title: Select a Cloud Runtime
steps.description:
Cloud runtimes provide powerful computational environments with CPU and GPU support. Execute your notebooks on scalable infrastructure without local setup.

[Select Runtime](command:datalayer.selectRuntime)

media.altText: Kernel source selection dialog with Datalayer Platform option

steps.title: Explore Datalayer Spaces
steps.description:
Datalayer Spaces are collaborative workspaces where teams can share notebooks, documents, and data. Access your spaces from the Explorer sidebar.

[Refresh Spaces](command:datalayer.refreshSpaces)

media.altText: Datalayer Spaces tree view showing notebooks and lexical documents

steps.title: Create a Collaborative Document
steps.description:
Lexical documents are Notion-like rich text editors with real-time collaboration. Perfect for reports, documentation, and team knowledge sharing.

[Create Local Lexical Document](command:datalayer.newLocalLexicalDocument)

[Create Lexical Document in Space](command:datalayer.newRemoteLexicalDocumentPrompt)

media.altText: Lexical rich text editor with formatting toolbar and document content
